status.description: Current T-0 confirmed by official or reliable sources.

launch_service_provider.name: SpaceX
launch_service_provider.description: Space Exploration Technologies Corp., known as SpaceX, is an American aerospace manufacturer and space transport services company headquartered in Hawthorne, California. It was founded in 2002 by entrepreneur Elon Musk with the goal of reducing space transportation costs and enabling the colonization of Mars. SpaceX operates from many pads, on the East Coast of the US they operate from SLC-40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station and historic LC-39A at Kennedy Space Center. They also operate from SLC-4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base, California, usually for polar launches. Another launch site is being developed at Boca Chica, Texas.

configuration.name: Falcon 9
configuration.description: Falcon 9 is a two-stage rocket designed and manufactured by SpaceX for the reliable and safe transport of satellites and the Dragon spacecraft into orbit. The Block 5 variant is the fifth major interval aimed at improving upon the ability for rapid reusability.

manufacturer.name: SpaceX
manufacturer.description: Space Exploration Technologies Corp., known as SpaceX, is an American aerospace manufacturer and space transport services company headquartered in Hawthorne, California. It was founded in 2002 by entrepreneur Elon Musk with the goal of reducing space transportation costs and enabling the colonization of Mars. SpaceX operates from many pads, on the East Coast of the US they operate from SLC-40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station and historic LC-39A at Kennedy Space Center. They also operate from SLC-4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base, California, usually for polar launches. Another launch site is being developed at Boca Chica, Texas.

program.name: Commercial Crew Program
program.description: The Commercial Crew Program (CCP) is a human spaceflight program operated by NASA, in association with American aerospace manufacturers Boeing and SpaceX. The program conducts rotations between the expeditions of the International Space Station program, transporting crews to and from the International Space Station (ISS) aboard Boeing Starliner and SpaceX Crew Dragon capsules, in the first crewed orbital spaceflights operated by private companies.

program.name: Commercial Resupply Services
program.description: Commercial Resupply Services (CRS) are a series of flights awarded by NASA for the delivery of cargo and supplies to the International Space Station.The first CRS contracts were signed in 2008 and awarded $1.6 billion to SpaceX for twelve cargo Dragon and $1.9 billion to Orbital Sciences for eight Cygnus flights, covering deliveries to 2016. The Falcon 9 and Antares rockets were also developed under the CRS program to deliver cargo spacecraft to the ISS.

landing.description: The Falcon 9 1st stage B1090 will land on ASDS ASOG after its 11th flight.

location.name: A Shortfall of Gravitas
location.description: The fourth ASDS barge, A Shortfall of Gravitas (ASOG) is currently used to recover Falcon 9 and Heavy boosters in the Altantic Ocean.

type.name: Autonomous Spaceport Drone Ship
type.description: An autonomous spaceport drone ship (ASDS) is an ocean-going vessel derived from a deck barge, outfitted with station-keeping engines and a large landing platform. Construction of such ships was commissioned by aerospace company SpaceX to allow for recovery of rocket first-stages at sea for high-velocity missions which do not carry enough fuel to return to the launch site after lofting spacecraft onto an orbital trajectory.

mission.name: Starlink Group 10-46
mission.description: A batch of 29 satellites for the Starlink mega-constellation - SpaceX's project for space-based Internet communication system.

agencies.name: SpaceX

It was founded in 2002 by entrepreneur Elon Musk with the goal of reducing space transportation costs and enabling the colonization of Mars. They also operate from SLC-4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base, California, usually for polar launches. Another launch site is being developed at Boca Chica, Texas.

location.name: Cape Canaveral SFS, FL, USA
location.description: Cape Canaveral Space Force Station (CCSFS) is an installation of the United States Space Force's Space Launch Delta 45, located on Cape Canaveral in Brevard County, Florida.

infoURLs.title: SpaceX
infoURLs.description: SpaceX designs, manufactures and launches advanced rockets and spacecraft. The company was founded in 2002 to revolutionize space technology, with the ultimate goal of enabling people to live on other planets.

vidURLs.title: GO! - SpaceX - Falcon 9 - Starlink 10-46 - SLC-40 - Cape Canaveral SFS - Space Affairs Live
vidURLs.description:
Launch Date:  March 17, 2026
Launch Time: 9:27 a.m. EDT, 1327 UTC, 14:27 CET
Launch Window:  Open until 10:26 a.m. EDT
Launch Site: SLC-40 - Cape Canaveral Space Force Station
Targeted Orbit: LEO (Low Earth Orbit) 
Launch Inclination: Northeast
Launch Status: Scheduled and announced

Mission: Starlink 10-46
Launch Provider: SpaceX
Launcher System: Falcon 9 (Booster 1090 - Turnaround 27 days)
Flight for the Booster: 11
Previous Flights of the Booster: 10 - SES O3b mPOWER-E, Crew-10, Bandwagon-3, mPOWER-D, CRS-33, and five Starlink missions
Droneship: A Shortfall Of Gravitas

Price: $69,75 million (without payload)
Diameter: 3,7 m
Height: 70 meters
Payload to Orbit: 22,800 LEO / 8,300 GTO
Lift-Off Thrust: 7,607 kilonewtons
Fairing: Diameter 5,2 meters / Height 13 meters
Stages: 2

Space...

vidURLs.title: Live: SpaceX Falcon 9 launches 29 Starlink satellites on North-East trajectory from Cape Canaveral
vidURLs.description:
Watch live coverage as SpaceX launches a Falcon 9 rocket carrying 29 satellites for Elon Musk's Starlink internet service. Liftoff from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station is scheduled for Tuesday, March 17, at 9:27 a.m. EDT (1327 UTC). The first-stage booster for this mission, 1090, launching for an 11th time, will land on SpaceX's drone ship 'A Shortfall of Gravitas', stationed in the Atlantic Ocean, roughly 8.5 minutes after leaving the launch pad.

The mission, designated Starlink 10-46, will take a north-easterly trajectory on departure from Florida's Space Coast.

Our live coverage with commentary from Spaceflight Now's Will Robinson-Smith will start about one hour prior to launch.

Videos like this are made possible by the support of our members. Join ...

vidURLs.title: SpaceX Falcon 9 launches Starlink 10-46
vidURLs.description:
Launch window: March 17th from 6:26AM to 10:26AM EDT (10:26-14:26 UTC)
Current T0: March 17th at 7:28AM EDT (11:28:00 UTC)

Mission: Falcon 9 launch of 29 Starlink v2 Mini satellites into orbit.
Launch location: Space Launch Complex 40 (SLC-40), Cape Canaveral Space Force Station, Earth.
Target orbit: Low-Earth Orbit (LEO); 253x264km 53.16º orbit
Booster: B1090-11; 29d 2h 26min 20s turnaround
Booster history: O3b mPOWER E, Crew-10, Bandwagon-3, SL 6-67, SL 10-18, O3b mPOWER D, CRS-33, SL 10-59, SL 6-79, SL 6-103.
Satellites launched: 155, will be 184 after this mission.
Booster recovery: A Shortfall Of Gravitas (ASOG)
Fairing recovery: Doug
Rocket trajectory: Northeast flying parallel to the US east coast
Payload mass: Approximately 17 tonnes.
Stats:
  - SpaceX's 643rd launch overall....

type.description: Launch director verifies go for propellant load
Start of liquid oxygen loading in the first stage
Start of propelland loading
Start of liquid oxygen loading in the second stage
Start of engine chilling
Fuel tanks are pressurized to flight levels
The onboard computer takes control over the countdown and runs last checks
Launch director verifies go for launch
Start of the engine ignition sequence
First upwards movement of the rocket
Maximum dynamic pressure
Cut-off of the main engine
Separation of the second stage from the first
First start of the second engine
Separation of the payload fairing
Start of the atmospheric entry burn
End of the atmospheric entry burn
Start of the first stage landing burn
Landing of the first stage
First cut-off of the second engine
Second start of the second engine
Second cut-off of the second engine
Deployment of the Starlink stack from the rocket

launch_service_provider.name: LandSpace
launch_service_provider.description: LandSpace is a Chinese private space launch company based in Beijing. It was founded in 2015 by Tsinghua University alumni Zhang Changwu.

configuration.name: Zhuque-2E
configuration.description: Zhuque-2E (E stands for “enhanced”) is a medium-sized rocket powered by liquid oxygen and methane capable of lifting 6,000 kg of payload into a 200 km low Earth orbit, or 4,000 kg of payload into a 500 km Sun-synchronous orbit. It differs from the baseline Zhuque-2 in using enhanced TQ-12A engines on the 1st stage with higher thrust and specific impulse than the original TQ-12, and a new TQ-15A engine on the 2nd stage that has a moveable nozzle.

manufacturer.name: LandSpace
manufacturer.description: LandSpace is a Chinese private space launch company based in Beijing. It was founded in 2015 by Tsinghua University alumni Zhang Changwu.

location.name: Jiuquan Satellite Launch Center, People's Republic of China
location.description: Jiuquan Satellite Launch Center is a Chinese spaceport located between the Ejin, Alxa, Inner Mongolia and Hangtian Town, Jinta County, Jiuquan, Gansu Province. It is part of the Dongfeng Aerospace City (Base 10).

results.name: Electron | Eight Days A Week (StriX Launch 8)

status.name: Go for Launch
status.description: Current T-0 confirmed by official or reliable sources.

net_precision.name: Minute
net_precision.description: The T-0 is accurate to the minute.

launch_service_provider.name: Rocket Lab
launch_service_provider.description: Rocket Lab is an American aerospace manufacturer with a wholly owned New Zealand subsidiary. The company develops lightweight, cost-effective commercial rocket launch services. The Electron Program was founded on the premise that small payloads such as CubeSats require dedicated small launch vehicles and flexibility not currently offered by traditional rocket systems. Its rocket, the Electron, is a light-weight rocket and is now operating commercially. The company is also producing a variety of spacecrafts and spacecrafts components.

configuration.name: Electron
configuration.description: Electron is a two-stage orbital expendable launch vehicle (with an optional third stage) developed by the American aerospace company Rocket Lab. Electron is a small-lift launch vehicle designed to launch small satellites and cubesats to sun-synchronous orbit and low earth orbit. The Electron is the first orbital class rocket to use electric-pump-fed engines, powered by the 9 Rutherford engines on the first stage. It is also used as a suborbital testbed (called HASTE) for hypersonics research.

manufacturer.name: Rocket Lab
manufacturer.description: Rocket Lab is an American aerospace manufacturer with a wholly owned New Zealand subsidiary. The company develops lightweight, cost-effective commercial rocket launch services. The Electron Program was founded on the premise that small payloads such as CubeSats require dedicated small launch vehicles and flexibility not currently offered by traditional rocket systems. Its rocket, the Electron, is a light-weight rocket and is now operating commercially. The company is also producing a variety of spacecrafts and spacecrafts components.

mission.name: Eight Days A Week (StriX Launch 8)
mission.description: Synthetic aperture radar satellite for Japanese Earth imaging company Synspective.

agencies.name: Synspective
agencies.description: Synspective was established with the aim to integrate SAR satellite technology in society through the agility of a start-up company. Synspective derives its name from the company concept of “Synthetic Data for Perspective on Sustainable Development.

location.name: Rocket Lab Launch Complex 1, Mahia Peninsula, New Zealand
location.description: Rocket Lab Launch Complex 1 is a commercial spaceport located close to Ahuriri Point at the southern tip of Māhia Peninsula, on the east coast of New Zealand's North Island. It is owned and operated by private spaceflight company Rocket Lab and supports launches of the company's Electron rocket for small satellites. With the launch of Electron on 25 May 2017, it became the first private spaceport to host an orbital launch attempt, and the first site in New Zealand to host an orbital launch attempt. With the Electron launch of 21 January 2018, it became the first private spaceport to host a successful orbital launch.

infoURLs.title: Eight Days A Week
infoURLs.description: The "Eight Days A Week" mission will be the eighth Synspective launch on Electron to build out the company's synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imaging constellation over Japan that provides data for urban development planning, construction and infrastructure monitoring, and disaster response.

status.name: To Be Confirmed
status.description: Awaiting official confirmation - current date is known with some certainty.

updates.comment: Delayed to next range window in February.
Scrubbed for the day due to pressurization valve issue.
Moved to NET December 2025 with possibly related NOTAMs/marine navigation warnings issued for December 6-19, to be confirmed as for Spectrum.

launch_service_provider.name: Isar Aerospace
launch_service_provider.description: Isar Aerospace is a German aerospace company based in Munich, Germany. The company was founded in 2018.

configuration.name: Spectrum
configuration.description: Spectrum is a small launch vehicle developed by German company Isar Aerospace.

manufacturer.name: Isar Aerospace
manufacturer.description: Isar Aerospace is a German aerospace company based in Munich, Germany. The company was founded in 2018.

mission.name: Onward and Upward
mission.description:
Second test flight of the Isar Spectrum launch vehicle. This launch will carry 5 cubesats and 1 non-separable experiment as part of European Space Agency (ESA)'s “Boost!” program:

* CyBEEsat (TU Berlin)
* TriSat-S (University of Maribor)
* Platform 6 (EnduroSat)
* FramSat-1 (NTNU)
* SpaceTeamSat1 (TU Wien Space Team)
* Let It Go (Dcubed, non-separable experiment)

location.name: Andøya Spaceport
location.description: Andøya Space, also named Andøya Space Center and formerly Andøya Rocket Range, is a rocket launch site, rocket range, and spaceport on Andøya island (the northernmost in the Vesterålen archipelago) in Andøy Municipality in Nordland county, Norway.

infoURLs.description:
The second flight of our ‘Spectrum’ launch vehicle marks a significant milestone serving as both the vehicle’s qualification mission and its first flight carrying payload.

 "Onward and Upward" symbolizes our relentless drive for progress and continuous improvement, reflecting our determination to b...

vidURLs.title: Livestream: "Onward and Upward" Mission of Isar Aerospace
vidURLs.description:
Mission Name: 'Onward and Upward'

Launch Date: Updates via https://isaraerospace.com/mission-updates-overview

Launch site: Andøya Space

The second flight of our ‘Spectrum’ launch vehicle marks a significant milestone serving as both the vehicle’s qualification mission and its first flight carrying payload.

"Onward and Upward" symbolizes our relentless drive for progress and continuous improvement, reflecting our determination to build upon the successes and lessons learned from our inaugural test flight.

The livestream is produced in collaboration with the @NASASpaceflight team.

More information on the Mission ‘Onward and Upward’ here: https://isaraerospace.com/mission-2

location.name: Of Course I Still Love You
location.description: The second ASDS barge, Of Course I Still Love You (OCISLY) services launches in the Pacific Ocean and was the site of the first landing of a SpaceX Falcon 9 first stage during CRS-8, the launch of a Dragon spacecraft to the International Space Station.

pad.name: Space Launch Complex 4E
pad.description:
Space Launch Complex 4 East (SLC-4E) is a launch site at Vandenberg Space Force Base, California, U.S.

The pad was previously used by Atlas and Titan rockets between 1963 and 2005. The pad was built for use by Atlas-Agena rockets, but was later rebuilt to handle Titan rockets.

location.name: Vandenberg SFB, CA, USA
location.description: Vandenberg Space Force Base is a United States Space Force Base in Santa Barbara County, California. Established in 1941, Vandenberg Space Force Base is a space launch base, launching spacecraft from the Western Range, and also performs missile testing. The United States Space Force's Space Launch Delta 30 serves as the host delta for the base, equivalent to an Air Force air base wing. In addition to its military space launch mission, Vandenberg Space Force Base also hosts space launches for civil and commercial space entities, such as NASA and SpaceX.

infoURLs.title: SpaceX
infoURLs.description: SpaceX designs, manufactures and launches advanced rockets and spacecraft. The company was founded in 2002 to revolutionize space technology, with the ultimate goal of enabling people to live on other planets.

updates.comment: Delayed into 2026 due to serious damage to the launch pad sustained during the launch of Soyuz MS-28 and long period required for repairs.

net_precision.name: Second

launch_service_provider.name: Russian Federal Space Agency (ROSCOSMOS)
launch_service_provider.description: The Roscosmos State Corporation for Space Activities, commonly known as Roscosmos, is the governmental body responsible for the space science program of the Russian Federation and general aerospace research. Soyuz has many launch locations the Russian sites are Baikonur, Plesetsk and Vostochny however Ariane also purchases the vehicle and launches it from French Guiana.

configuration.name: Soyuz 2.1a
configuration.description:
The Soyuz 2.1A converted the flight control system from analog to digital, which allowed launch from fixed platforms. It also allowed big fairings and payloads.
It is currently used for crewed Soyuz and Progress flights to the ISS.

manufacturer.name: Progress Rocket Space Center
manufacturer.description: Progress Rocket Space Centre, formerly known as TsSKB-Progress, is a space science and aerospace research company which is known for manufacturing launch vehicles and satellites. Most notably, Progress Rocket Space Centre is the manufacturer of Soyuz launch vehicles.

mission.name: Progress MS-33 (94P)
mission.description: Progress resupply mission to the International Space Station.

agencies.name: Russian Federal Space Agency (ROSCOSMOS)
agencies.description: The Roscosmos State Corporation for Space Activities, commonly known as Roscosmos, is the governmental body responsible for the space science program of the Russian Federation and general aerospace research. Soyuz has many launch locations the Russian sites are Baikonur, Plesetsk and Vostochny however Ariane also purchases the vehicle and launches it from French Guiana.

location.name: Baikonur Cosmodrome, Republic of Kazakhstan
location.description: The Baikonur Cosmodrome is a spaceport operated by Russia within Kazakhstan. Located in the Kazakh city of Baikonur, it is the largest operational space launch facility in terms of area. All Russian crewed spaceflights are launched from Baikonur.

program.name: International Space Station
program.description: The International Space Station programme is tied together by a complex set of legal, political and financial agreements between the sixteen nations involved in the project, governing ownership of the various components, rights to crewing and utilization, and responsibilities for crew rotation and resupply of the International Space Station. It was conceived in 1984 by President Ronald Reagan, during the Space Station Freedom project as it was originally called.

configuration.description: Falcon 9 is a two-stage rocket designed and manufactured by SpaceX for the reliable and safe transport of satellites and the Dragon spacecraft into orbit. The Block 5 variant is the fifth major interval aimed at improving upon the ability for rapid reusability.

manufacturer.name: SpaceX
manufacturer.description: Space Exploration Technologies Corp., known as SpaceX, is an American aerospace manufacturer and space transport services company headquartered in Hawthorne, California. It was founded in 2002 by entrepreneur Elon Musk with the goal of reducing space transportation costs and enabling the colonization of Mars. SpaceX operates from many pads, on the East Coast of the US they operate from SLC-40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station and historic LC-39A at Kennedy Space Center. They also operate from SLC-4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base, California, usually for polar launches. Another launch site is being developed at Boca Chica, Texas.

program.name: Commercial Crew Program
program.description: The Commercial Crew Program (CCP) is a human spaceflight program operated by NASA, in association with American aerospace manufacturers Boeing and SpaceX. The program conducts rotations between the expeditions of the International Space Station program, transporting crews to and from the International Space Station (ISS) aboard Boeing Starliner and SpaceX Crew Dragon capsules, in the first crewed orbital spaceflights operated by private companies.

program.name: Commercial Resupply Services
program.description: Commercial Resupply Services (CRS) are a series of flights awarded by NASA for the delivery of cargo and supplies to the International Space Station.The first CRS contracts were signed in 2008 and awarded $1.6 billion to SpaceX for twelve cargo Dragon and $1.9 billion to Orbital Sciences for eight Cygnus flights, covering deliveries to 2016. The Falcon 9 and Antares rockets were also developed under the CRS program to deliver cargo spacecraft to the ISS.

launcher.details: Falcon 9 Block 5 booster first used for the Crew-6 mission.

landing.description: The Falcon 9 1st stage B1078 will land on ASDS ASOG after its 27th flight.

location.name: A Shortfall of Gravitas
location.description: The fourth ASDS barge, A Shortfall of Gravitas (ASOG) is currently used to recover Falcon 9 and Heavy boosters in the Altantic Ocean.

type.name: Autonomous Spaceport Drone Ship
type.description: An autonomous spaceport drone ship (ASDS) is an ocean-going vessel derived from a deck barge, outfitted with station-keeping engines and a large landing platform. Construction of such ships was commissioned by aerospace company SpaceX to allow for recovery of rocket first-stages at sea for high-velocity missions which do not carry enough fuel to return to the launch site after lofting spacecraft onto an orbital trajectory.

mission.name: Starlink Group 10-62
mission.description: A batch of 29 satellites for the Starlink mega-constellation - SpaceX's project for space-based Internet communication system.

agencies.name: SpaceX
agencies.description: Space Exploration Technologies Corp., known as SpaceX, is an American aerospace manufacturer and space transport services company headquartered in Hawthorne, California. It was founded in 2002 by entrepreneur Elon Musk with the goal of reducing space transportation costs and enabling the colonization of Mars. SpaceX operates from many pads, on the East Coast of the US they operate from SLC-40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station and historic LC-39A at Kennedy Space Center. They also operate from SLC-4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base, California, usually for polar launches. Another launch site is being developed at Boca Chica, Texas.

location.name: Cape Canaveral SFS, FL, USA
location.description: Cape Canaveral Space Force Station (CCSFS) is an installation of the United States Space Force's Space Launch Delta 45, located on Cape Canaveral in Brevard County, Florida.

infoURLs.title: SpaceX
infoURLs.description: SpaceX designs, manufactures and launches advanced rockets and spacecraft. The company was founded in 2002 to revolutionize space technology, with the ultimate goal of enabling people to live on other planets.

type.description: Launch director verifies go for propellant load

Start of liquid oxygen loading in the second stage
Start of engine chilling
The onboard computer takes control over the countdown and runs last checks
Fuel tanks are pressurized to flight levels

Maximum dynamic pressure
Cut-off of the main engine
Separation of the second stage from the first

results.name: Electron | Daughter Of The Stars (LEO-PNT Pathfinder A)

status.name: To Be Determined
status.description: Current date is a placeholder or rough estimation based on unreliable or interpreted sources.

net_precision.name: Day
net_precision.description: The T-0 is expected on the given day.

launch_service_provider.name: Rocket Lab
launch_service_provider.description: Rocket Lab is an American aerospace manufacturer with a wholly owned New Zealand subsidiary. The company develops lightweight, cost-effective commercial rocket launch services. The Electron Program was founded on the premise that small payloads such as CubeSats require dedicated small launch vehicles and flexibility not currently offered by traditional rocket systems. Its rocket, the Electron, is a light-weight rocket and is now operating commercially. The company is also producing a variety of spacecrafts and spacecrafts components.

configuration.name: Electron
configuration.description: Electron is a two-stage orbital expendable launch vehicle (with an optional third stage) developed by the American aerospace company Rocket Lab. Electron is a small-lift launch vehicle designed to launch small satellites and cubesats to sun-synchronous orbit and low earth orbit. The Electron is the first orbital class rocket to use electric-pump-fed engines, powered by the 9 Rutherford engines on the first stage. It is also used as a suborbital testbed (called HASTE) for hypersonics research.

manufacturer.name: Rocket Lab
manufacturer.description: Rocket Lab is an American aerospace manufacturer with a wholly owned New Zealand subsidiary. The company develops lightweight, cost-effective commercial rocket launch services. The Electron Program was founded on the premise that small payloads such as CubeSats require dedicated small launch vehicles and flexibility not currently offered by traditional rocket systems. Its rocket, the Electron, is a light-weight rocket and is now operating commercially. The company is also producing a variety of spacecrafts and spacecrafts components.

mission.name: Daughter Of The Stars (LEO-PNT Pathfinder A)
mission.description:
The European Space Agency (ESA)'s LEO-PNT (Low Earth Orbit Positioning, Navigation and Timing) demonstrator mission will feature a 10-satellite constellation demonstration mission that will assess how a low Earth orbit fleet of satellites can work in combination with the Galileo and EGNOS constellations in higher orbits that provide Europe’s own global navigation system.

This launch will lift 2 “Pathfinder A” satellites built by Thales Alenia Space and GMV to a 510 km altitude Low Earth Orbit.

agencies.name: European Space Agency
agencies.description:
The European Space Agency is an intergovernmental organisation of 22 member states. Established in 1975 and headquartered in Paris, France, ESA has a worldwide staff of about 2,000 employees.

ESA's space flight programme includes human spaceflight (mainly through participation in the International Space Station program); the launch and operation of unmanned exploration missions to other planets and the Moon; Earth observation, science and telecommunication; designing launch vehicles; and maintaining a major spaceport, the Guiana Space Centre at Kourou, French Guiana.

location.name: Rocket Lab Launch Complex 1, Mahia Peninsula, New Zealand
location.description: Rocket Lab Launch Complex 1 is a commercial spaceport located close to Ahuriri Point at the southern tip of Māhia Peninsula, on the east coast of New Zealand's North Island. It is owned and operated by private spaceflight company Rocket Lab and supports launches of the company's Electron rocket for small satellites. With the launch of Electron on 25 May 2017, it became the first private spaceport to host an orbital launch attempt, and the first site in New Zealand to host an orbital launch attempt. With the Electron launch of 21 January 2018, it became the first private spaceport to host a successful orbital launch.

status.name: Go for Launch
status.description: Current T-0 confirmed by official or reliable sources.

net_precision.name: Minute
net_precision.description: The T-0 is accurate to the minute.

launcher.details: Falcon 9 booster, with a special “100” logo in lieu of the usual serial number style painted on the booster.

landing.description: The Falcon 9 first stage B1100 will land on ASDS OCISLY after its 4th flight.

location.name: Of Course I Still Love You
location.description: The second ASDS barge, Of Course I Still Love You (OCISLY) services launches in the Pacific Ocean and was the site of the first landing of a SpaceX Falcon 9 first stage during CRS-8, the launch of a Dragon spacecraft to the International Space Station.

mission.name: Starlink Group 17-17
mission.description: A batch of 25 satellites for the Starlink mega-constellation - SpaceX's project for space-based Internet communication system.

agencies.name: SpaceX

pad.name: Space Launch Complex 4E
pad.description:
Space Launch Complex 4 East (SLC-4E) is a launch site at Vandenberg Space Force Base, California, U.S.

The pad was previously used by Atlas and Titan rockets between 1963 and 2005. The pad was built for use by Atlas-Agena rockets, but was later rebuilt to handle Titan rockets.

location.name: Vandenberg SFB, CA, USA
location.description: Vandenberg Space Force Base is a United States Space Force Base in Santa Barbara County, California. Established in 1941, Vandenberg Space Force Base is a space launch base, launching spacecraft from the Western Range, and also performs missile testing. The United States Space Force's Space Launch Delta 30 serves as the host delta for the base, equivalent to an Air Force air base wing. In addition to its military space launch mission, Vandenberg Space Force Base also hosts space launches for civil and commercial space entities, such as NASA and SpaceX.
